item.description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, President Trump says “the weaponization fund is a great idea” and if Republicans “don't get it approved, I'd be disappointed.” Asked whether anyone who attacked police officers on January 6 should get taxpayer money, Trump says, “I wouldn't be inclined to say so, but I have to see it.”

media:content.media:title: Trump says ‘I’d pay’ anti-weaponization fund applicants ‘the kind of money they deserve’
media:content.media:description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, President Trump says “the weaponization fund is a great idea” and if Republicans “don't get it approved, I'd be disappointed.” Asked whether anyone who attacked police officers on January 6 should get taxpayer money, Trump says, “I wouldn't be inclined to say so, but I have to see it.”

item.title: Trump says ‘I’d pay’ anti-weaponization fund applicants ‘the kind of money they deserve’

item.description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, President Trump addresses the economy amid the war with Iran and says Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh is “fantastic,” adding, “I want him to do whatever he wants” when it comes to setting interest rates.

media:content.media:title: Trump on new Fed Chair Warsh: ‘I don’t want to have a big influence on him’
media:content.media:description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, President Trump addresses the economy amid the war with Iran and says Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh is “fantastic,” adding, “I want him to do whatever he wants” when it comes to setting interest rates.

item.title: Trump on new Fed Chair Warsh: ‘I don’t want to have a big influence on him’

item.description: Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker joins Sunday TODAY’s Willie Geist to share key takeaways her exclusive interview with President Donald Trump about the war in Iran, its impact on the economy and his claims of voter fraud in California and the 2020 election. Kristen says, “I pressed him on his promise not to get the United States engaged in foreign wars. He said ‘This is not going to be a forever war.’ He insists it’s not a quagmire.”

media:content.media:title: Trump on Pressure to End Iran War With Peace Deal
media:content.media:description: Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker joins Sunday TODAY’s Willie Geist to share key takeaways her exclusive interview with President Donald Trump about the war in Iran, its impact on the economy and his claims of voter fraud in California and the 2020 election. Kristen says, “I pressed him on his promise not to get the United States engaged in foreign wars. He said ‘This is not going to be a forever war.’ He insists it’s not a quagmire.”

item.title: Trump on Pressure to End Iran War With Peace Deal

item.dc:creator: Peter Nicholas
item.description: President Donald Trump did not rule out the government paying people who were charged with assaulting police officers during the Jan. 6 attack on the U.S.

media:content.media:text: Meet The Press- President Trump
media:content.media:description: "Meet the Press" moderator Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5 at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wis.

item.title: Trump doesn’t rule out giving Jan. 6 rioters who attacked police ‘anti-weaponization’ fund payouts

item.dc:creator: Jane C. Timm
item.description: Digging into some of the president’s claims on the Iran war, the Jan. 6 riots, California’s primary elections and more.

media:content.media:text: "Meet the Press" Host Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5, 2026 at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wisc.
media:content.media:description: "Meet the Press" moderator Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5, 2026, at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wis.

item.title: Fact-checking Trump’s interview with NBC News’ ‘Meet the Press’

item.dc:creator: NBC News
item.description: Trump spoke about the war with Iran, the “anti-weaponization” fund, the economy, recent elections in California and more in June 2026 interview.

media:content.media:text: Meet The Press- President Trump
media:content.media:description: "Meet the Press" Host Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5, 2026 at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wisc.

item.title: Read the transcript: President Donald Trump interviewed by NBC News’ ‘Meet the Press’ moderator Kristen Welker

item.description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, President Trump talks about the economy, the ongoing war with Iran and the possible path to peace.

media:content.media:title: Full interview: Trump says Iran ‘is not an endless war’ as conflict reaches 100 days
media:content.media:description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, President Trump talks about the economy, the ongoing war with Iran and the possible path to peace.

item.title: Full interview: Trump says Iran ‘is not an endless war’ as conflict reaches 100 days

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: Former “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley accused CBS News chief Bari Weiss of tilting coverage in favor of how President Donald Trump characterized events in Minnesota and described a newsroom in turmoil under her leadership during an emotional New York Times interview published Sunday

media:content.media:text: Scott Pelley seated on set
media:content.media:description: CBS News' former "60 Minutes" correspondent Scott Pelley.

item.title: Scott Pelley alleges CBS leadership pushed for a more pro-Trump framing

item.dc:creator: Freddie Clayton
item.description: LONDON — For the second time in a week, a top Trump administration official has publicly criticized Europe over migration levels, the latest in a series of attacks aimed at political leaders on the continent

media:content.media:text: France D-Day
media:content.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth delivers a speech in Colleville-sur-Mer, Normandy, on June 6 to commemorate the 82nd anniversary of the D-Day landings.

item.title: Hegseth attacks Europe on immigration at D-Day event, continuing trend for Trump officials

item.description: A new luxury development in Albania, backed by Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump, is drawing backlash from locals citing environmental concerns. NBC News' Raf Sanchez reports.

media:content.media:title: Backlash in Albania over plans for new luxury resort backed by Kushner
media:content.media:description: A new luxury development in Albania, backed by Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump, is drawing backlash from locals citing environmental concerns. NBC News' Raf Sanchez reports.

item.title: Backlash in Albania over plans for new luxury resort backed by Kushner

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: All eyes will be on the World Cup host cities this summer, but perhaps none more so than Los Angeles, which is also preparing to host the 2028 Summer Olympics

media:content.media:text: Workers affix FIFA World Cup 2026 logos and signage on a fence outside of SoFi Stadium
media:content.media:description: Workers affix FIFA World Cup 2026 logos and signage at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Calif., on May 26.

media:content.media:text: The interior of SoFi Stadium
media:content.media:description: Workers affix FIFA World Cup 2026 logos and signage at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Calif., on May 26.

item.title: World Cup gives Los Angeles a chance to showcase Olympic readiness

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.dc:creator: NBC Chicago
item.description: Former Chicago Bulls star and NBA broadcaster Stacey King has died at the age of 59, the team announced Sunday

media:content.media:text: Stacey King smiles
media:content.media:description: Former Chicago Bulls player Stacey King during a 20th anniversary recognition ceremony of the Bulls first NBA Championship in 1991 at the United Center in Chicago in 2011.

media:content.media:text: Stacey King holds a basketball on the court
media:content.media:description: Former Chicago Bulls player Stacey King during a 20th anniversary recognition ceremony of the Bulls first NBA Championship in 1991 at the United Center in Chicago in 2011.

item.title: Stacey King, a 3-time NBA champion with the Chicago Bulls, dies at 59

item.dc:creator: NBC News
item.description: Trump spoke about the war with Iran, the “anti-weaponization” fund, the economy, recent elections in California and more in June 2026 interview.

media:content.media:text: Meet The Press- President Trump
media:content.media:description: "Meet the Press" Host Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5, 2026 at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wisc.

item.title: Read the transcript: President Donald Trump interviewed by NBC News’ ‘Meet the Press’ moderator Kristen Welker

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: Former “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley accused CBS News chief Bari Weiss of tilting coverage in favor of how President Donald Trump characterized events in Minnesota and described a newsroom in turmoil under her leadership during an emotional New York Times interview published Sunday

media:content.media:text: Scott Pelley seated on set
media:content.media:description: CBS News' former "60 Minutes" correspondent Scott Pelley.

item.title: Scott Pelley alleges CBS leadership pushed for a more pro-Trump framing

item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
item.description: A four-goal lead in what has been a wacky, compelling and highly entertaining Stanley Cup Final nearly wasn’t enough for the Golden Knights on Saturday night

media:content.media:text: Mitch Marner celebrates with Shea Theodore on the ice
media:content.media:description: Vegas Golden Knights right wing Mitch Marner celebrates with defenseman Shea Theodore after scoring a goal against the Carolina Hurricanes during the second period in game three of the 2026 Stanley Cup Final on Saturday.

item.title: Golden Knights blow 4-goal lead but beat Hurricanes in 2OT to take Game 3 of the Stanley Cup

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
item.description: Kimi Antonelli won a dramatic Formula 1 Monaco Grand Prix on Sunday, extending his championship lead to a commanding 66 points after a race twice interrupted by crashes and a red flag

media:content.media:text: Andrea Kimi Antonelli of Italy driving the Mercedes AMG Petronas F1 Team W17 on track
media:content.media:description: Andrea Kimi Antonelli of Italy driving the Mercedes AMG Petronas F1 Team W17 on track during the F1 Grand Prix of Monaco on Sunday.

item.title: Kimi Antonelli wins F1 Monaco Grand Prix after crashes and red flag drama

item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
item.description: Pope Leo XIV honored Spain’s centuries-old tradition of religious devotion on Sunday as a “school of faith” for today, as he presided over a Mass before a million people and highlighted one of the most iconic expressions of Spanish popular piety with a procession over flower-petal carpets

media:content.media:text: Pope Leo waves
media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV arrives in the popemobile at Plaza de Cibeles for a Holy Mass and Corpus Christi procession in Madrid, Spain, on Sunday.

media:content.media:text: Flower petal beds can be seen on the street in between rows of people crowded on the sidewalks
media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV arrives in the popemobile at Plaza de Cibeles for a Holy Mass and Corpus Christi procession in Madrid, Spain, on Sunday.

media:content.media:text: Aerial view of an outdoor mass attended by a large crowd
media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV arrives in the popemobile at Plaza de Cibeles for a Holy Mass and Corpus Christi procession in Madrid, Spain, on Sunday.

item.title: 1 million people flood Madrid streets to see the pope’s flower-carpeted procession

item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
item.description: TIJUANA, México — Iran’s soccer team arrived in Mexico on Sunday morning for training ahead of the World Cup, before three group matches in the United States later this month

media:content.media:text: Ehsan Haji Safi walks on the airport tarmac carrying a suitcase, people exit a plane via stairs behind him
media:content.media:description: Members of the Iranian national soccer team including captain Ehsan Haji Safi arrive at Tijuana International Airport, in Tijuana, Mexico, on Sunday.

item.title: Iran’s soccer team arrives in Mexico for training ahead of the World Cup

item.dc:creator: Megan Lebowitz
item.description: President Donald Trump criticized the possibility of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates, but said in an interview on NBC News’ “Meet the Press” that he wants Fed Chair Kevin Warsh “to do whatever he wants.”“Kevin is fantastic, and I want him to do whatever he wants,” Trump told moderator Kristen Welker.

media:content.media:text: "Meet the Press" Host Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5, 2026 at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wisc.
media:content.media:description: "Meet the Press" Host Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5, 2026 at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wisc.

item.title: Trump says Fed chair should ‘do whatever he wants’ but criticizes possible rate hikes

item.dc:creator: Freddie Clayton
item.description: LONDON — For the second time in a week, a top Trump administration official has publicly criticized Europe over migration levels, the latest in a series of attacks aimed at political leaders on the continent

media:content.media:text: France D-Day
media:content.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth delivers a speech in Colleville-sur-Mer, Normandy, on June 6 to commemorate the 82nd anniversary of the D-Day landings.

item.title: Hegseth attacks Europe on immigration at D-Day event, continuing trend for Trump officials

item.title: Fact-checking Trump’s interview with NBC News’ ‘Meet the Press’

item.dc:creator: Jonathan Allen
item.description: President Donald Trump said the U.S. will work with Iran to retrieve and destroy its highly enriched uranium if he is able to cut a deal with Tehran to end the war.

media:content.media:text: "Meet the Press" Host Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5, 2026 at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wisc.
media:content.media:description: "Meet the Press" moderator Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5 at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wis.

item.title: Trump says the U.S. will work with Iran to destroy its uranium if they can make a deal

item.dc:creator: Peter Nicholas
item.description: President Donald Trump did not rule out the government paying people who were charged with assaulting police officers during the Jan. 6 attack on the U.S.

media:content.media:text: Meet The Press- President Trump
media:content.media:description: "Meet the Press" moderator Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5 at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wis.

item.title: Trump doesn’t rule out giving Jan. 6 rioters who attacked police ‘anti-weaponization’ fund payouts

media:content.media:description: Workers affix FIFA World Cup 2026 logos and signage at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Calif., on May 26.

media:content.media:text: The interior of SoFi Stadium
media:content.media:description: Workers affix FIFA World Cup 2026 logos and signage at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Calif., on May 26.

item.title: World Cup gives Los Angeles a chance to showcase Olympic readiness
